The code for the helper function would look something like:

private int backtrack(int mask, int[] nums, Map<Integer, Integer> memo) {
    if (memo.containsKey(mask)) {
        return memo.get(mask);
    }
    int n = nums.length;
    int max = 0;
    int count = Integer.bitCount(mask);
    int step = (count / 2) + 1;
    List<Integer> available = new ArrayList<>();
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        if ((mask & (1 << i)) == 0) {
            available.add(i);
        }
    }
    for (int i = 0; i < available.size(); i++) {
        for (int j = i + 1; j < available.size(); j++) {
            int a = available.get(i);
            int b = available.get(j);
            int newMask = mask | (1 << a) | (1 << b);
            int currentGCD = gcd(nums[a], nums[b]);
            int currentScore = step * currentGCD;
            int nextScore = backtrack(newMask, nums, memo);
            max = Math.max(max, currentScore + nextScore);
        }
    }
    memo.put(mask, max);
    return max;
}

But wait, the base case is when there are no available elements (available is empty). Then, return 0. Which is handled automatically because the for loops won't run, and max remains 0.

public int maxScore(int[] nums) {
    Map<Integer, Integer> memo = new HashMap<>();
    return backtrack(0, nums, memo);
}

But in Java, the code must be in the Solution class. So, the code would look like:

class Solution {
    public int maxScore(int[] nums) {
        Map<Integer, Integer> memo = new HashMap<>();
        return backtrack(0, nums, memo);
    }

    private int backtrack(int mask, int[] nums, Map<Integer, Integer> memo) {
        if (memo.containsKey(mask)) {
            return memo.get(mask);
        }
        int max = 0;
        int count = Integer.bitCount(mask);
        int step = (count / 2) + 1;
        List<Integer> available = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) {
            if ((mask & (1 << i)) == 0) {
                available.add(i);
            }
        }
        for (int i = 0; i < available.size(); i++) {
            for (int j = i + 1; j < available.size(); j++) {
                int a = available.get(i);
                int b = available.get(j);
                int newMask = mask | (1 << a) | (1 << b);
                int currentScore = step * gcd(nums[a], nums[b]);
                int nextScore = backtrack(newMask, nums, memo);
                max = Math.max(max, currentScore + nextScore);
            }
        }
        memo.put(mask, max);
        return max;
    }

    private int gcd(int a, int b) {
        while (b != 0) {
            int temp = b;
            b = a % b;
            a = temp;
        }
        return a;
    }
}

solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int maxScore(int[] nums) {
        java.util.Map<Integer, Integer> memo = new java.util.HashMap<>();
        return backtrack(0, nums, memo);
    }

    private int backtrack(int mask, int[] nums, java.util.Map<Integer, Integer> memo) {
        if (memo.containsKey(mask)) {
            return memo.get(mask);
        }
        int max = 0;
        int count = java.lang.Integer.bitCount(mask);
        int step = (count / 2) + 1;
        java.util.List<Integer> available = new java.util.ArrayList<>();
        for (int i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) {
            if ((mask & (1 << i)) == 0) {
                available.add(i);
            }
        }
        for (int i = 0; i < available.size(); i++) {
            for (int j = i + 1; j < available.size(); j++) {
                int a = available.get(i);
                int b = available.get(j);
                int newMask = mask | (1 << a) | (1 << b);
                int currentScore = step * gcd(nums[a], nums[b]);
                int nextScore = backtrack(newMask, nums, memo);
                max = Math.max(max, currentScore + nextScore);
            }
        }
        memo.put(mask, max);
        return max;
    }

    private int gcd(int a, int b) {
        while (b != 0) {
            int temp = b;
            b = a % b;
            a = temp;
        }
        return a;
    }
}
```
efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(N^2 * 2^N) where N is the length of nums (2n)
space complexity: O(2^N) for memoization and recursion stack
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Uses bitmask dynamic programming with memoization to explore all possible pairings. For each state (bitmask), it tries all possible pairs of available elements, computes their GCD multiplied by the current step, and recursively finds the optimal score. Strengths: Efficient for small n (up to 7). Weaknesses: Exponential time and space, not feasible for larger n.
